nodes.text: Hi, sorry — here, let me just — *a sheaf of papers slides off the counter* Oh god. Sorry. I'm Sandra Kowalski. Monthly pickup. Ignore the chaos.

responses.text: No trouble at all. Monthly pickup for Kowalski?

nodes.text: That's me. The metformin. Sandra Kowalski, K-O-W-A-L-S-K-I. I never know if there's an H in there and there isn't.

responses.text: Got it. I'll need your ID.

nodes.text: End of semester. I have forty-three sets of papers due by Friday and I am not making that deadline. One of my kids turned in a book report on a book they definitely didn't read and honestly? I respect the commitment. *gathers papers from counter*

responses.text: How many kids do you teach?
Let me get your prescription going. ID?

nodes.text: Twenty-seven this year. Usually it's closer to twenty-four but they combined two sections and I haven't forgiven the administration. I take the metformin in part because of that decision.

responses.text: That's a lot. Can I see your ID?

nodes.text: Yes — here — hold on, it's in here somewhere. *rummages in tote bag* Why do I have three different pens and no wallet. There. Oh, and can I grab some ibuprofen? The big bottle. For the headaches.

responses.text: It's in the third aisle. I'll start on your prescription.

nodes.text: Thank you. You're a saint. The kids say I complain too much about the grading but they don't know. They don't know.
